questions.question: 1. What is the binary representation of the decimal number 31?
2. Which of the following C statements sets the 0th bit of n to 1?

explanations.a: Correct. Bitwise OR with 1 sets the least significant bit to 1.
explanations.b: Incorrect. Bitwise AND with 1 extracts the least significant bit.
explanations.c: Incorrect. Bitwise XOR with 1 toggles the least significant bit.
explanations.d: Incorrect. Left shift multiplies n by 2.

questions.question: 3. What is the result of left shifting the binary number 101 by 2 positions?

explanations.a: Incorrect. 10100 is 20, but shifting by 2 gives 101000.

questions.question: 4. Which of the following is the correct way to check if the 3rd bit (from right, 0-indexed) of n is set in C?

explanations.a: Correct. Bitwise AND with (1 << 3) checks if the 3rd bit is set.
explanations.b: Incorrect. Bitwise OR sets the bit, not checks it.
explanations.c: Incorrect. Bitwise XOR toggles the bit.
explanations.d: Incorrect. '&&' is a logical AND, not bitwise.

questions.question: 5. What is the minimum number of bits required to represent the decimal number 1023 in binary?

explanations.a: Incorrect. 9 bits can represent up to 511 only.
explanations.c: Incorrect. 11 bits can represent up to 2047, but 10 bits are enough for 1023.
explanations.d: Incorrect. 8 bits can represent up to 255 only.

questions.question: 6. Which of the following C statements clears the 2nd bit (from right, 0-indexed) of n?

explanations.a: Correct. Bitwise AND with the negation of (1 << 2) clears the 2nd bit.
explanations.b: Incorrect. Bitwise OR sets the bit.
explanations.c: Incorrect. Bitwise XOR toggles the bit.
explanations.d: Incorrect. Left shift multiplies n by 4.

questions.question: 7. Which of the following is the binary representation of the decimal number 512?
8. Which of the following best describes the effect of the XOR (^) operator in binary arithmetic?

answers.a: It sets a bit if both bits are 1
answers.b: It sets a bit if both bits are 0
answers.c: It sets a bit if the bits are different
answers.d: It always sets the bit to 1

explanations.c: Correct. XOR sets a bit if the bits are different.
explanations.a: Incorrect. AND sets a bit if both are 1.
explanations.b: Incorrect. NOR sets a bit if both are 0.
explanations.d: Incorrect. This is not true for XOR.

questions.question: 9. What is the result of 14 ^ 9 in C? (where ^ is bitwise XOR)

explanations.d: Incorrect. 3 is not the result of 14 ^ 9.

questions.question: 10. Which of the following best describes the purpose of using binary representation in computers?

answers.a: It is easier for humans to read
answers.b: It is the only way to represent numbers
answers.c: It matches the on/off nature of digital circuits

explanations.c: Correct. Binary matches the on/off (high/low) states of digital circuits.
explanations.a: Incorrect. Decimal is easier for humans.
explanations.b: Incorrect. Numbers can be represented in other bases too.
